questions.question:
How does the author's use of figurative language help readers understand the explicit and implicit feelings that the narrator is expressing? 

Respond to this question in the form of a paragraph that includes a claim, evidence, and explanation.

responses.content:
The explicit is that Rachel shows her feelings when Mrs.Price gave her the sweater Rachel as saying “ I put my head down on the desk and bury my face in my stupid clown-sweater arms.” Rachel  thinks that the sweater is ugly smells like cottage cheese that made her face hot and spit on accident according to the text it says ” My face all hot and spit coming out of my mouth because I can't stop the little animal noises from coming out of me until there aren't any more tears left in my eyes.” This show that Rachel hates the sweater.
The implicit part shows that Rachel really wants to take off the sweater because when Mrs.Price hands her the sweater she knew that sweater was not a regular sweater because is sweaty, is hot, is smells bad so she didn’t want to put it on her body, but Mrs.Price forced Rachel to wear it Rachel was disgusted when she put the sweater on so she felt shamed when she put the sweater on she said she felt like a clown and she felt like she not her age because she started to cry in front the whole classroom in paragraph 19 it says “That's when everything I've been holding in since this morning, since when Mrs. Price put the sweater on my desk, finally lets go, and all of a sudden I'm crying in front of everybody. I wish I was invisible but I'm not. I'm eleven and it's my birthday today and I'm crying like I'm three in front of everybody.” This shows that Rachel doesn’t feel 11 she feels 3 years old because she was crying in front of everybody.

The explicit is that Rachel shows her feelings when Mrs.Price gave her the sweater Rachel as saying “ I put my head down on the desk and bury my face in my stupid clown-sweater arms.” Rachel  thinks that the sweater is ugly smells like cottage cheese that made her face hot and spit on accident according to the text it says ” My face all hot and spit coming out of my mouth because I can't stop the little animal noises from coming out of me until there aren't any more tears left in my eyes.” This show that Rachel hates the sweater.
The implicit part shows that Rachel really wants to take off the sweater because when Mrs.Price hands her the sweater she knew that sweater was not a regular sweater because is sweaty, is hot, is smells bad so she didn’t want to put it on her body, but Mrs.Price forced Rachel to wear it Rachel was disgusted when she put the sweater on so she felt shamed when she put the sweater on she said she felt like a clown and she felt like she not her age because she started to cry in front the whole classroom in paragraph 19 it says “That's when everything I've been holding in since this morning, since when Mrs. Price put the sweater on my desk, finally lets go, and all of a sudden I'm crying in front of everybody. I wish I was invisible but I'm not. I'm eleven and it's my birthday today and I'm crying like I'm three in front of everybody.” This shows that Rachel doesn’t feel 11 she feels 3 years old because she was crying in front of everybody. And Rachel felt upset and she wished to be 102 because she wanted too respond to Mrs.Price that the sweater did not belong to her  because that old people are wise in paragraph 5 “ Today I wish I was one hundred and two instead of eleven because if I was one hundred and two I'd have known what to say when Mrs. Price put the red sweater on my desk.” This proved that Rachel wished to be 102 years old so that she could respond good to Mrs.Price and not 11 years old because she can’t respond good to Mrs.Price because then she wouldn’t stutter to Mrs.Price

questions.question:
Read the following passage from paragraph 10:

“I closed my eyes. I saw Dad standing on a stage, his head bowed, his name written in large black letters, and then crossed out in red ink, on a sign hanging from his neck. I saw myself standing in the middle of the stage, facing thousands of people, condemning Dad for his crimes, raising my fist to lead the chant, ‘Down with Jiang Xi-reng.’ I saw Dad looking at me hopelessly, tears on his face.”

What does this suggest about Ji-li's relationship with her father?

responses.content: That Ji-li Jiang is doing well on her studies and participated in educational reform

The question asks how Papa feels about their decision—his attitude toward the men, not his emotions about the overall situation.

Look closely at what Papa says in paragraphs 29 and 33. After the men explain why they're quitting, what does Papa say that shows how he feels about their choice?

It looks like you’re trying to figure out exactly what the question is asking. 😊 The question wants you to explain how Papa feels about the men’s decision—not just what happened, but what Papa thinks about their choice. Try to find a word or phrase in the text that shows Papa’s attitude, like if he understands, accepts, or respects their decision, and then explain why he feels that way. 📚
Papa respects both Mr.Avery and Mr.Lainer because they worked so hard but they have less opertuinties

To improve your response, use this frame: Papa feels ____ about their decision because ____.

For the first blank, find a word in paragraphs 29 or 33 that shows Papa's attitude toward the men (not anger or indifference). For the second blank, use Papa's explanation in paragraph 33 about why the men had to make this choice.
